content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates multiple specific, timely reads that change the conversation. In turn 4, the coach names the linguistic caveat ('I notice you went straight back to is it okay') — naming the rehearsed deflection in real time. In turn 6, the coach names a somatic/vocal shift ('notice what happened in your voice just then. It softened') and distinguishes the said from the shown (asking permission vs. describing love). In turn 10, the coach names the defensive structure itself ('the selfish question is the place where you stop, so you don't have to feel that one') — a pattern read that surfaces the avoidance function. In turn 12, the coach explicitly brings attention to the body ('What do you notice in your body'). Reads consistently precede questions, are specific rather than generic, and land at moments where they shift the field.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Refuses verdict and pivots to the want, but no explicit read named yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the linguistic caveat / return-to-the-gate pattern explicitly before asking the next question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks vocal/somatic softening in real time and distinguishes the said from the shown."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Question without an explicit read, but well-timed."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the defensive function of the 'selfish' question — a pattern-level read of avoidance."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Brings attention to the body, inviting somatic tracking at the threshold moment."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Affirms the want vs. should distinction; not a new read."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Reads are specific (linguistic, vocal, somatic, defensive-function) and consistently precede inquiry."
}
